methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The valuation universe consists of eight private companies operating in this space, including later-stage peers such as Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in the narrow sector definition.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains five same-sector peers, but none satisfy the filtering criteria for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation-tier similarity that would be required to establish a reliable comparable set of three or more companies. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models and development timelines across the broader universe.

Without a sufficiently sized peer cohort meeting standard filtering thresholds, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range based on comparable company metrics. Alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis (if unit economics and revenue projections were available) or venture capital method analysis (if target return assumptions and exit scenarios were specified)—would be required to establish a defensible valuation. The key uncertainty is whether Airship Industries' specific technology platform, customer traction, or capital efficiency materially distinguishes it from the peer set, which would be necessary to justify any premium or discount relative to later-stage peers.
